name: Dr Kaitlyn Davis
focus: Environmental archaeology • Geospatial analysis • UAV and photogrammetry • Historic Preservation
bio: Kaitlyn E. Davis is an Assistant Professor of Anthropology at Northern Arizona University and a Registered Professional Archaeologist whose research focuses on the archaeology of the Southwestern United States. She specializes in community-engaged and environmental archaeology, with particular interests in Pueblo societies, trade interactions, and changes in settlement patterns and land use. Dr. Davis integrates fieldwork, geospatial technologies, and remote sensing methods—such as UAV-based imaging and photogrammetry—to better understand past human-environment relationships and cultural landscapes. She directs the Environmental Archaeology Lab and Ceramic Analysis Lab at NAU and is a core faculty member in the Radiant Center for Remote Sensing and is the Southwest Hub co-lead for the NSF-supported Center for Braiding Indigenous Knowledge and Science. Dr. Davis earned her Ph.D. in Anthropology from the University of Colorado Boulder in 2022 and has experience across academic, museum, and cultural resource management settings.
